text: Top-down attention works through a mechanism researchers call predictive coding. The brain does not passively receive the world; it generates constant predictions about incoming stimuli and then processes only the difference — the 'prediction error.' The prefrontal cortex issues these predictions downward to sensory cortices, effectively pre-loading expectations that determine what we notice. This means attention is fundamentally anticipatory. Karl Friston, whose free energy principle formalizes this process, argues that perception is controlled hallucination: what we see is largely what the PFC expected to see, refined by just enough sensory data to correct its model.
question: According to Karl Friston's free energy principle as described in the passage, what does the brain primarily process?

options.text: The full stream of incoming sensory data from the environment
Emotional responses generated by the limbic system
The difference between predictions and actual stimuli — the prediction error
Signals forwarded upward from the sensory cortex to the PFC
